title: Sideshow Bobs Last Gleaming

jokes.text: Your generous donations have made this our slipperiest, slimiest slobstacle course ever!
jokes.setup: Krusty thanking people for donating canned food to a food drive
jokes.punchline: The food drive donations are being used for a disgusting obstacle course
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining 'obstacle' with 'slob' while subverting charity expectations

jokes.text: How can we afford it? It's rancid!
jokes.setup: Krusty describing expensive béarnaise sauce for the obstacle course
jokes.punchline: The show can only afford it because it's gone bad
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty character moment - cheap production values justified with twisted logic

jokes.text: Krusty, please! It burns!
jokes.setup: The disgusting obstacle course Krusty described
jokes.punchline: Sideshow Mel's genuine suffering in the course
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe humor payoff to the setup - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Oh, just think, Lise. That's our pickle brine burning Sideshow Mel.
jokes.setup: Sideshow Mel screaming in pain from the obstacle course
jokes.punchline: Homer takes pride in their family's contribution to the suffering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - misplaced pride in causing harm, delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Ah, Westminster Abbey. Edward the Confessor himself could not have done better.
jokes.setup: Bob in prison, presumably doing some mundane task
jokes.punchline: He compares his prison work to building Westminster Abbey
jokes.explanation: Classic Sideshow Bob pretension - elevating mundane prison tasks with grandiose historical references

jokes.text: This is the way we mop the floor, mop the floor mop the floor
jokes.setup: Prison inmates doing chores
jokes.punchline: Snake singing a children's song while mopping
jokes.explanation: Mild character contrast between tough criminal and innocent children's song

jokes.text: My foolish capering destroyed more young minds than syphilis and pinball combined.
jokes.setup: Bob lamenting his television career
jokes.punchline: Comparing children's TV to syphilis and pinball as corrupting influences
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bob line - perfect intellectual pretension with absurd moral panic references. Economy and character integration are flawless

jokes.text: that omnidirectional sludge pump droning and burping-
jokes.setup: Bob describing television
jokes.punchline: Describing TV as an 'omnidirectional sludge pump'
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor that's both visceral and technical - perfectly captures Bob's intellectual disdain

jokes.text: Look here! That's enough now! I own 60% of that network!
jokes.setup: Bob insulting television
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony reveals he owns most of the network being insulted
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke revealing Fat Tony's unexpected business interests

jokes.text: There. That's the last condom wrapper.
jokes.setup: Cleaning up the air show grounds
jokes.punchline: Revealing what kind of debris they're finding
jokes.explanation: Mild shock humor about what's found at public events - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Informally now and by affidavit later, time permitting.
jokes.setup: Bob objecting to prison work detail
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily formal legal language for simple complaint
jokes.explanation: Classic Bob - making mundane situations sound like legal proceedings. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Buzz-cut Alabamians spewing colored smoke from their whizz jets to the strains of 'Rock You Like a Hurricane'? What kind of country-fried rube is still impressed by that?
jokes.setup: Discussion of the air show
jokes.punchline: Bob's elaborate, pretentious dismissal of military air shows
jokes.explanation: Masterful Bob characterization - cultural elitism meets specific, vivid imagery. 'Country-fried rube' is perfectly dismissive

jokes.text: During the Gulf War, she destroyed 70 mosques. And her name is Lisa too.
jokes.setup: Lisa wanting to meet female stealth bomber pilot
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions massive destruction of religious buildings, connects through shared name
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about war crimes delivered innocently. The name connection adds absurdity.

jokes.text: I want to see some birds get sucked into the engines, rare ones.
jokes.setup: Kids expressing excitement about the air show
jokes.punchline: Bart wants to see endangered species killed by jet engines
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart darkness - the 'rare ones' detail elevates simple morbidity to specific cruelty

jokes.text: This year I'm making ear plugs out of biscuit dough.
jokes.setup: Discussion of the loud air show
jokes.punchline: Cletus's improvised ear protection from food
jokes.explanation: Good Cletus character moment - resourceful hillbilly ingenuity with absurd materials

jokes.text: Friend? You mean you two aren't knocking boots?
jokes.setup: Granddaughter introducing a male friend
jokes.punchline: Grandmother immediately assumes sexual relationship
jokes.explanation: Standard inappropriate elderly person humor - gets the reaction but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Ever do the back-seat mambo, Craigie?
jokes.setup: Continuing the inappropriate questioning
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic term for sex combined with overly familiar nickname
jokes.explanation: Follow-up to previous joke - 'back-seat mambo' is a nice euphemism but overall predictable

jokes.text: TV's bottomless chum bucket has claimed Vanessa Redgrave!
jokes.setup: Seeing respected actress on trashy TV
jokes.punchline: Bob's horror expressed through maritime metaphor
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bob pretension - 'bottomless chum bucket' is a vivid, disgusted metaphor for TV

jokes.text: Now I'm gonna haul ass to Lollapalooza.
jokes.setup: Bob lamenting highbrow actress on TV
jokes.punchline: The actress using very lowbrow language
jokes.explanation: Perfect juxtaposition - respected British actress using crude American slang validates Bob's point

jokes.text: I have three medals for this.
jokes.setup: Being mocked for failing to guard the parking lot
jokes.punchline: Military officer proudly defending his parking lot expertise
jokes.explanation: Good military bureaucracy satire - treating mundane tasks as heroic achievements

jokes.text: Well, if anyone asks, uh, I beat him to death, okay?
jokes.setup: Learning that dangerous criminal Sideshow Bob has escaped
jokes.punchline: Wiggum immediately wants to lie about killing him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - preferring to fabricate success rather than do the work

jokes.text: Five tires? Am I seeing things?
jokes.setup: Looking at a Harrier jet
jokes.punchline: Homer focuses on the mundane detail of tire count
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - missing the impressive technology to fixate on car-like features

jokes.text: Five tires? Am I seeing things?
jokes.setup: Looking at military aircraft
jokes.punchline: Homer is confused by unusual number of landing gear
jokes.explanation: Homer focusing on mundane detail while missing bigger picture. Good character moment.

jokes.text: it's so well-designed, even a child could fly it. - Can I fly it? - Of course you cannot.
jokes.setup: Military guide praising the jet's ease of use
jokes.punchline: Immediately refusing when a child asks to try
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline exposing the meaninglessness of the salesman's pitch

jokes.text: Free Duff? Viva life!
jokes.setup: Someone discovering free beer
jokes.punchline: Overwrought celebration using Spanish and mismatched enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about excessive celebration over beer. Not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Take that, Mom! Take that, Dad! Send me to a psychiatrist, will you? Take that, Dr. Sally Waxler!
jokes.setup: Bart playing with military equipment
jokes.punchline: Acting out revenge fantasies against family and therapist
jokes.explanation: Good Bart character moment revealing family dysfunction through play. Specific therapist name adds realism

jokes.text: This is Colonel Leslie 'Hap' Hapablap. If you don't open that door, I'll tear you up like a Kleenex at a snot party!
jokes.setup: Colonel demanding entry to his private washroom
jokes.punchline: Elaborate, crude metaphor for violence
jokes.explanation: Creative military bluster - 'snot party' is both disgusting and unexpectedly festive

jokes.text: If you don't open that door, I'll tear you up like a Kleenex at a snot party!
jokes.setup: Colonel threatening intruder
jokes.punchline: Elaborate simile comparing violence to tissue destruction at disgusting gathering
jokes.explanation: Fantastic original simile. 'Snot party' is gross but funny concept. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Sweet Enola Gay, son! I'm gonna come in there and corpse you up!
jokes.setup: Colonel's continued threats
jokes.punchline: Using atomic bomber as exclamation, plus 'corpse' as a verb
jokes.explanation: More military character building - dark historical reference as casual oath, plus creative verb usage

jokes.text: Oh, got my knuckles all lined up for nothing.
jokes.setup: Intruder has apparently escaped
jokes.punchline: Colonel disappointed he prepared for violence but can't use it
jokes.explanation: Funny image of preparing fists for fighting. Shows character's violent inclination.

jokes.text: At Kitty Hawk in 1903... Charles Lindbergh flew it 15 miles on a thimbleful of corn oil. Single-handedly won us the Civil War, it did.
jokes.setup: Cletus explaining the Wright Brothers' plane
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong historical facts jumbled together
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cletus ignorance - mixing up Wright Brothers, Lindbergh, Civil War timing, and fuel types

jokes.text: I pieced it together mostly from sugar packets.
jokes.setup: Being asked about his historical knowledge
jokes.punchline: Learning history from restaurant sugar packet facts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy and character - explains Cletus's garbled knowledge perfectly with specific, believable detail

jokes.text: Get down there with the chamois triple-time!
jokes.setup: Military emergency about runway foam
jokes.punchline: Urgent military language for car-washing equipment
jokes.explanation: Mild military satire - treating mundane cleaning with combat urgency

jokes.text: Get moving or I'll tear you up like a Kleenex at a, uh, snot party.
jokes.setup: Colonel threatening soldier again
jokes.punchline: Reusing his earlier colorful threat but stumbling slightly
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier threat with slight hesitation showing he's reusing his go-to phrase

jokes.text: They didn't have any aspirin, so I got you some cigarettes.
jokes.setup: Marge mentioning a headache
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely illogical solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - helpful intent with completely wrong solution. Economy is excellent

jokes.text: what a day for an air show! Not a cloud in the sky.! Except perhaps... a mushroom cloud.
jokes.setup: Standard upbeat air show introduction
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning nuclear annihilation
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor misdirection - cheerful weather report takes nuclear turn

jokes.text: Except perhaps... a mushroom cloud.
jokes.setup: Commenting on clear skies at air show
jokes.punchline: Ominous hint about nuclear explosion
jokes.explanation: Good ominous foreshadowing. Contrast between beautiful weather and destruction threat.

jokes.text: Say! Did somebody say box kites? - No.!
jokes.setup: Announcer enthusiastically introducing box kites
jokes.punchline: Crowd's flat rejection of his enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline about misreading audience enthusiasm

jokes.text: The common box kite was originally used as a means of drying wet string.
jokes.setup: Educational segment about box kites
jokes.punchline: Completely useless and probably false historical fact
jokes.explanation: Absurdist fake fact that sounds plausible but is completely pointless

jokes.text: Why jerk my neck around like a goon when Tyranno-Vision decides what I should look at?
jokes.setup: Lisa asking why he's not watching the air show
jokes.punchline: Homer's made-up term for television control over attention
jokes.explanation: 'Tyranno-Vision' is a brilliant Homer coinage combining dinosaur and tyranny - shows surprising media awareness

jokes.text: Surely he's not talking about VH1?
jokes.setup: Sideshow Bob threatening to eliminate television
jokes.punchline: Moe's specific concern about music television
jokes.explanation: Good specific reference humor - Moe's priorities revealed through which channel he's worried about

jokes.text: Why, we could revive the lost arts of conversation... and scrimshaw.
jokes.setup: Bob promoting life without television
jokes.punchline: Pairing conversation with obscure nautical carving art
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bob pretension - pairing universal human activity with incredibly niche hobby

jokes.text: Go back to Massachusetts, pinko!
jokes.setup: Bob's intellectual anti-TV speech
jokes.punchline: Crowd's knee-jerk political dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good satire of reflexive anti-intellectualism and red-baiting

jokes.text: By the way, I'm aware of the irony of appearing on TV in order to decry it. So don't bother pointing that out.
jokes.setup: Bob's anti-television broadcast
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging the obvious irony before anyone else can
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor that's perfectly in character - Bob's intellectual superiority includes anticipating obvious criticisms

jokes.text: You must be very proud, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Marge saying her children are trapped in nuclear threat zone
jokes.punchline: Military person responds as if it's an honor
jokes.explanation: Dark humor treating danger as achievement. Good misreading of situation.

jokes.text: We'll find that head case faster than Garfield finds lasagna.
jokes.setup: Promising to catch Sideshow Bob quickly
jokes.punchline: Comparison to cartoon cat's food obsession
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference humor - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Oh. I'm sorry. My- My wife thought that was gangbusters.
jokes.setup: His Garfield joke falling flat
jokes.punchline: Awkward explanation blaming his wife
jokes.explanation: Good cringe follow-up to failed joke - shows character trying to save face

jokes.text: We have searched every square inch of this base... and all we have found is porno, porno, porno!
jokes.setup: Searching military base for dangerous criminal
jokes.punchline: Only finding pornography everywhere
jokes.explanation: Repetition creates emphasis but the base military porn joke is fairly predictable

jokes.text: Whoa! Whoa-ho-ho! Let's not go nuts!
jokes.setup: Suggestion to sacrifice television to save lives
jokes.punchline: Krusty thinks eliminating TV is the crazy idea, not the bomb threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty priorities - TV matters more than human lives to the entertainer

jokes.text: Would it really be worth living in a world without television? I think the survivors would envy the dead.
jokes.setup: Discussion of life without TV
jokes.punchline: Executive thinks TV-less world is worse than death
jokes.explanation: Good dark hyperbole that reveals character priorities - echoes nuclear war rhetoric

jokes.text: dogs that were mistakenly issued major credit cards and others who weren't so lucky.
jokes.setup: News anchor listing career highlights
jokes.punchline: Treating dogs without credit cards as the unfortunate ones
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - implying dogs should have credit cards and pitying those without

jokes.text: Some decent local marmalade, for one. Oh, well, next time.
jokes.setup: Bob regretting not making more demands
jokes.punchline: Specific, mundane desire for quality jam
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bob pretension - nuclear terrorism for better breakfast spreads. 'Next time' is casually ominous

jokes.text: Wait. If I could only stay on the air... I'd have 100% of the audience. Think of the ratings.
jokes.setup: All TV being eliminated except his show
jokes.punchline: Krusty immediately seeing the business opportunity
jokes.explanation: Great Krusty character moment - finding the business angle in potential tragedy

jokes.text: Do you know what really frosts my Kelvinator?
jokes.setup: Colonel expressing frustration
jokes.punchline: Using appliance brand name in expression about being angry
jokes.explanation: Creative military slang using refrigerator brand for 'what annoys me' - good wordplay

jokes.text: I'd rather take an order from Bill Clinton than hear that guy's snooty, high-toned voice again, sir.
jokes.setup: Military frustration with Sideshow Bob
jokes.punchline: Comparing civilian annoyance to taking orders from Democratic president
jokes.explanation: Decent political humor showing military bias, though the reference is somewhat dated

jokes.text: Tight, binding underwear? - Helium!
jokes.setup: Lisa asking what makes voices high
jokes.punchline: Lisa gives embarrassing answer, Bart gives correct one
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection joke - Lisa's overthinking vs. Bart's simple correct answer

jokes.text: Blasted helium! Shoo! Shoo! Shoo! Shoo! That's better.
jokes.setup: Bob's voice being affected by helium in the blimp
jokes.punchline: Trying to shoo away the gas like an annoying pet
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of Bob treating gas like a pest - cute but not exceptional

jokes.text: I'll be beaming out 11 watts of wackiness!
jokes.setup: Krusty broadcasting from civil defense shack
jokes.punchline: Specific low wattage paired with alliterative enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between technical specificity and vague showbiz enthusiasm

jokes.text: Uh, and, uh, former president, Ike Eisenhower!
jokes.setup: Krusty listing his improvised show guests
jokes.punchline: Dead president somehow available for comedy show
jokes.explanation: Absurd desperation booking - dead president as comedy guest shows how unprepared Krusty is

jokes.text: Uh, and, uh, former president, Ike Eisenhower!
jokes.setup: Krusty continuing to promise guest stars
jokes.punchline: Claims to have dead president as guest
jokes.explanation: Krusty's desperation leads to impossible guest booking. Eisenhower reference shows his panic.

jokes.text: How naive of me to think a mere atom bomb could fell the chattering Cyclops!
jokes.setup: Bob's plan failing as TV continues
jokes.punchline: Poetic metaphor for television as mythological monster
jokes.explanation: 'Chattering Cyclops' is brilliant Bob metaphor - combines classical education with visceral disgust for TV

jokes.text: 'Best before November 1959.' Damn it, Bob! There were plenty of brand-new bombs. But you had to go for that retro '50s charm.
jokes.setup: Finding Bob's nuclear weapon
jokes.punchline: Criticizing the bomb's vintage like it's a fashion choice
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic treating nuclear weapons like consumer goods with style preferences

jokes.text: and his sister, Lisa, to whom I'm fairly indifferent.
jokes.setup: Bob identifying Bart as his arch-nemesis
jokes.punchline: Dismissing Lisa with polite indifference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bob politeness in evil situation - formal dismissal of non-rival is charmingly specific

jokes.text: Really? What type of smart? Book smart? Because there are a lot of people who are book smart. But it takes a very special type of genius to-
jokes.setup: Lisa flattering Bob to stall
jokes.punchline: Bob falling for obvious flattery and getting specific about his intelligence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bob's ego overriding his intelligence - classic villain mistake

jokes.text: Oh, not the Harrier! We've got a war tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Bob escaping in military aircraft
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of scheduled warfare
jokes.explanation: Dark humor treating war like a scheduled appointment - good military mindset satire

jokes.text: God bless the idiot-proof Air Force.
jokes.setup: Bob successfully operating military aircraft
jokes.punchline: Thanks the military for making their weapons so easy to steal and use
jokes.explanation: Great ironic comment on military security. 'Idiot-proof' backfires by helping criminals.

jokes.text: Hell! Not the Wright Brothers' plane. The Smithsonian's gonna have my ass on a platter.
jokes.setup: Chase involving historical aircraft
jokes.punchline: More worried about museum than public safety
jokes.explanation: Good priority joke - bureaucratic concerns override actual danger

jokes.text: And I had a blimp fall on me! And I was in an atomic blast! But I'm okay now!
jokes.setup: Bart listing his adventures
jokes.punchline: Treating near-death experiences as casual accomplishments
jokes.explanation: Good Bart resilience joke - life-threatening events treated as exciting stories

jokes.text: Well, I wrecked the gate. But you don't hear me bragging. Now let's roll!
jokes.setup: Homer responding to Bart's adventure stories
jokes.punchline: Bragging about minor property damage while claiming not to brag
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer competitive moment - one-upping son's survival story with property damage

jokes.text: Back before every Joe Sweat Sock could wedge himself behind a lunch tray and jet off to Raleigh-Durham.
jokes.setup: Bob lamenting modern aviation
jokes.punchline: Elaborate insult about democratization of air travel
jokes.explanation: 'Joe Sweat Sock' is brilliant Bob elitism - specific mundane destination adds to the disdain

jokes.text: Bogie's air speed not sufficient for intercept. Suggest we get out and walk.
jokes.setup: Military jets chasing old Wright Brothers plane
jokes.punchline: Suggesting they abandon high-tech aircraft for walking
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist military logic - high-tech solution defeated by low-tech problem

jokes.text: It's The Stingy and Battery Show.! They bite and light and bite and light and bite
jokes.setup: Krusty introducing improvised cartoon replacement
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun-based cartoon premise with repetitive action
jokes.explanation: Decent satire of cheap cartoon production and pun-based naming

jokes.text: What the hell is that? A lawn mower?
jokes.setup: Wright Brothers plane approaching
jokes.punchline: Mistaking historical aircraft for garden equipment
jokes.explanation: Simple comparison joke showing how primitive early aircraft looked

jokes.text: Uh, sorry. Uh, we don't normally drive these in the Air Force.
jokes.setup: Air Force pilot crashing into building
jokes.punchline: Apologetic explanation for incompetence with flying machine
jokes.explanation: Mild incompetence humor - Air Force not knowing how to fly is basic irony

jokes.text: What is your major malfunction, Sideshow Bob?
jokes.setup: Capturing Sideshow Bob
jokes.punchline: Military jargon applied to psychological analysis
jokes.explanation: Basic military language joke - functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: What is your major malfunction, Sideshow Bob?
jokes.setup: Soldier confronting captured Bob
jokes.punchline: Uses military drill sergeant terminology to question criminal's psychology
jokes.explanation: Standard military speak applied to situation. Functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: How ironic. My crusade against television has come to an end so formulaic... it could have spewed from the PowerBook of the laziest Hollywood hack.
jokes.setup: Bob's defeat in conventional TV-like fashion
jokes.punchline: Recognizing his story follows tired television tropes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary - Bob recognizes he's in a TV show with TV logic. Perfect character integration with his anti-TV stance

jokes.text: Here we go again. - Here we go again.
jokes.setup: Vanessa Redgrave saying 'haul ass to Lollapalooza' again on TV
jokes.punchline: Simpson parents recognizing the repetitive nature of TV
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier Redgrave moment, showing cyclical nature of TV content

tmdb_overview: After Sideshow Bob escapes from prison, the Springfield Air Show is the setting for his ill-fated vendetta against television and Krusty the Clown.
insight: Sideshow Bob's military obsession sustains 78 jokes across a tight 22-minute arc.
